title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After returning a key to a real estate agent, Frankie travels by subway toward Piccadilly Square. While reading a newspaper, she notices a report regarding an accident involving Sir John Milkingdon and the Astrola, a yacht formerly owned by the late millionaire John Savage. The report notes that the yacht's designer was killed and that Savage committed suicide. Frankie recalls previous information suggesting that Allen Castles was a friend of Savage and that Mrs. Rivington believed Castles' return to England was linked to Savage's death. Suspecting that Savage's suicide may be connected to her current investigation, Frankie decides to examine Savage's will.

Frankie visits the law firm Spragg-Spragg-Jackson & Spragg, Esq. to meet Mr. Spragg, an affable senior lawyer rumored to know the secrets of London's aristocracy. When Frankie asks to view John Savage's will, Mr. Spragg expresses surprise and warns her against involvement in suspicious matters, revealing that he has recently been the victim of a deliberate impersonation. Frankie admits to the impersonation, claiming it was a joke, and deflects further blame by implying a young Duke was involved, which causes Mr. Spragg to soften his demeanor. 

During their conversation, Frankie presents a letter from Mrs. Rivington stating that Allen Castles mentioned traveling to Chipping Sodbury and expressed interest in the "Maltrevor" case. Mr. Spragg expresses suspicion regarding the seriousness of the matter, specifically concerning the Maltrevor case or his client, Mr. Castles.
